checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=86515703.14 (2013-12-31); ratio to revenue ~9.0%
SalesRevenueGoodsNet=960976947.21 (2013 annual)
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=256592128.72 (2016-12-31); ratio to revenue ~13.4%, a step-change vs 2013-2014 that was not fully reversed by 2017-2018 (~12.2%-12.6%)
Revenues=1991691603.70 (2016 annual)

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill=245386607.86 (2015-03-31), a step-up consistent with acquisition activity that also drove increases in PP&E, inventory and AR concurrently
OtherAssetsNoncurrent=51022556.81 (2018-12-31) vs Assets=1749262036.90 (2018-12-31), a modest proportion of total assets

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-19886678.07 (2017 annual)
NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=97851027.22 (2017 annual)
NetIncomeLoss=-19079837.71 (2018 annual)
NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=258877077.54 (2018 annual), roughly 2.6x the FY2016 level of 151075706.03 even though NI turned negative
DepreciationAndAmortization=74901817.22 (2018 annual), insufficient to explain the ~278M gap between NI and OCF

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=477407192.31 (2017-12-31), ~23.2% of Revenues=2055936370.18 (2017 annual), the highest ratio in the dataset
InventoryNet=420107516.42 (2018-12-31), reverting to ~19.6% of Revenues=2139975098.15 (2018 annual)
GrossProfit=927748558.09 (2017 annual); GrossProfit=964446607.99 (2018 annual), margin essentially flat at ~45% despite the inventory swing

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-936231.51 (2017 Q1); NetIncomeLoss=-5071803.43 (2017 Q2); NetIncomeLoss=22351703.10 (2017 Q3); NetIncomeLoss=-36230346.23 (2017 Q4)
NetIncomeLoss=-12461933.65 (2018 Q1); NetIncomeLoss=-39371172.18 (2018 Q2); NetIncomeLoss=31015141.14 (2018 Q3); NetIncomeLoss=1738126.98 (2018 Q4)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AccountsPayableCurrent=231217864.83 (2017-12-31); AccountsPayableCurrent=231125560.32 (2018-12-31), roughly flat despite ~4% revenue growth
AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=122320377.21 (2017-12-31); AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=140276077.79 (2018-12-31), growing faster than revenue

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K filed 2013-02-25 followed immediately by a 10-K/A filed 2013-02-26

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Data series span from FY2009 (10-Q filed 2010-08-05) through FY2018 (10-K filed 2019-02-25), covering roughly 10 years of quarterly and annual data across core line items

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Large non-cash charges (depreciation/amortization, possible impairments or below-the-line interest/other losses) and favorable working-capital timing (inventory drawdown, receivable/payable timing) appear to be depressing GAAP net income in 2017-2018 while operating cash flow remained strong and rose sharply, creating a persistent and widening gap between earnings and cash generation not fully explained by disclosed D&A alone.

Receivables grew from ~9% of revenue (2013-2014) to a sustained ~11-13% of revenue (2015-2018), a step-change that could reflect looser credit terms, delayed cash collection, or revenue recognized ahead of collectability during the company's rapid, largely acquisition- and debt-funded expansion, raising the possibility of aggressive revenue recognition during the growth phase.

rationale_evidence.location: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent & Revenues, FY2016 comparative in FY2018 10-K

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Inventory-to-revenue ratio spiked to its highest level in the dataset (~23%) in 2017 before reverting to ~20% in 2018, without a corresponding dip or improvement in gross margin (steady ~45%), which is atypical if the buildup reflected demand softness or obsolescence risk that should normally pressure margins upon liquidation or write-down.

top_signals: Persistent and widening divergence between GAAP net income (losses of ~$19.9M in 2017 and ~$19.1M in 2018) and operating cash flow (rising to $258.9M in 2018), far exceeding what disclosed D&A can explain
Step-change increase in accounts receivable relative to revenue from ~9% (2013-2014) to a sustained ~11-13% (2015-2018)
Inventory-to-revenue ratio spike to ~23% in 2017 (highest in dataset) followed by reversion in 2018 without a corresponding gross margin swing
Recurring SEC UPLOAD/CORRESP correspondence rounds (2011-2012 and 2017-2018) and a same-day-following 10-K/A amendment in Feb 2013, suggesting periods of heightened regulatory scrutiny of the filings
